settings.title: We Now Offer Same-Day Leak Callouts
settings.excerpt: A second van means we can reach most leaks within the working day rather than booking you in for next week.
settings.body:
A leak does not wait politely for an appointment, so neither should we. From this month Pipeworks runs a second van dedicated to same-day callouts.

If you ring before noon with an active leak, we aim to be at your door the same afternoon. Burst pipes, failing valves, and overflowing cisterns all count. If you are not sure that yours qualifies, describe it on the phone and we will tell you straight away.

What counts as a same-day job

  • A burst or visibly leaking pipe
  • A valve or fitting that has started weeping or failed
  • An overflowing cistern or a tank you cannot shut off

The second van carries the same spares as the first, so most leaks are fixed in the single visit rather than made safe and booked back. While we are with you, we will also point out the valve to reach for next time, so a repeat of the same problem is less frightening.

The early slots fill fast, so call as soon as you spot trouble rather than waiting to see if it stops on its own. It rarely does, and a small drip left overnight has a habit of becoming a ceiling stain by morning.

Routine jobs like new taps and bathroom fits still book in the normal way, and that wait has actually shortened now the urgent work has its own van. So the change helps everyone, not just the emergencies.
